rulings.Case Title: VICTOR PEREZ VS. DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 2, 2014, Line 3: PETITIONER VICTOR PEREZ' Hearing On Writ Of Mandate. Writ of mandate denied. In reviewing the record, the trial court exercises its independent judgment, while affording a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings. (See Lake v. Reed (1997) 16 Cal.4th 448, 456; Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 816-817.) The weight of the evidence supports the hearing officer's findings that the peace officer had reasonable cause to stop Petitioner's vehicle and probable cause to make a lawful arrest. Further, the weight of the evidence supports the hearing officer's finding that Petitioner's blood alcohol level was at or above .08% while Petitioner was driving his motor vehicle. The weight of the evidence also supports the finding that Mr. Zehnder's testimony is not credible, and does not rebut the presumption of Vehicle Code § 23152. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Pltffs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 2, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S MOTION For Terminating, Issue And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO SEEK THE REQUESTED RELIEF FROM THE TRIAL DEPARTMENT. Plaintiffs Meier and Selig-Farney served further verified responses to Form Interrogatories 6.2 and 6.3 on or about December 6, 2013. The responses were all hedged with the proviso that the manifestations identified in response to Form Interrogatories 6.2 and 6.3 "include but are not necessary limited to loss of sleep, anxiety, headaches, and stomach discomfort." Plaintiffs' responses to Form Interrogatory 6.2 are evasive and incomplete because they do not identify each injury. Plaintiffs' responses to Form Interrogatory 6.3 are evasive and incomplete because the interrogatory asks for information about each complaint that the Plaintiffs' attribute to the incident. However, given that Defendant had not deposed Plaintiffs prior to the date of the amended verified responses, and the discovery cutoff appears to have been extended in connection with the Court's continuance of the trial date to February 24, 2013, Defendant still has the opportunity to elicit, during Plaintiffs' depositions, further information about the manifestations Plaintiffs attribute to their emotional distress. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA RETTGER VS. REGENTS OF UNIV OF CA AT SF MED CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 2, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTs NANCY LOPEZ & THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted, in part, with leave to amend. Plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages regarding her "general negligence" and "intentional tort" claims is barred under CCP § 425.13 because the damages arise out of alleged professional negligence of a health care provider in her capacity as a practitioner. (See Central Pathology Service Medical Clinic, Inc. v. Superior Court (1992) Cal.4th 181, 189-191.) The Court strikes the following from the Complaint: p.3, §14(a)(2) (seeking punitive damages) and the exemplary damages attachment in its entirety. The rest of the motion to strike is denied. rulings.Case Title: SANDRA RETTGER VS. REGENTS OF UNIV OF CA AT SF MED CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 2, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTs NANCY LOPEZ & THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege sufficient facts to state a cause of action for concealment or intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff is given 20 days leave to amend to plead facts, if she can do so in good faith, that she would have acted differently had she known of the concealed fact and/or that she justifiably relied on Defendant's misrepresentation. The Court does not consider the arguments raised by Defendants for the first time in their Reply brief (i.e., statutory immunity from intentional tort causes of action, and "uncertainty"). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
